steps.title: Use the required factor
steps.content:
For the expression to simplify to $x+b$, the numerator must factor as:
$$(x-b)(x+b)$$

steps.title: Expand the product
steps.content:
Use the difference of squares:
$$(x-b)(x+b)=x^2-b^2$$
So $x^2-c$ must match $x^2-b^2$, which means $c=b^2$.

steps.title: Choose a square value
steps.content: Since $b$ is a positive integer, $c$ must be a perfect square. Among the choices, $9=3^2$ is the only perfect square. The answer is A.
